mechanism_narrative: A surprise increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market, which reduces aggregate demand and corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative equity returns as investors price in a higher probability of recession.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE recently rose to 4.0%, a notable increase.
failure_condition: In more than 50% of triggered instances, the S&P 500 posts positive 20-day forward returns, indicating the market is ignoring the negative labor signal.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high inflation, even if decelerating, keeps nominal rates elevated and pressures the central bank to maintain a restrictive policy stance. This compresses equity valuation multiples via a higher discount rate, leading to negative forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Current data shows CPI YoY ~3.3% and FEDFUNDS at 5.33%
failure_condition: The S&P 500 exhibits a positive Sharpe ratio over 60-day periods when the trigger condition is met for more than two consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real retail sales indicates robust consumer spending, which is the primary driver of the US economy. This strength translates directly into higher corporate revenues and earnings, supporting positive equity market performance.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between 3-month changes in real retail sales (RSAFS deflated by CPIAUCSL) and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained downturn in industrial production is a leading indicator of a manufacturing recession and broader economic weakness. This signals falling demand for goods and lower capital investment, leading to negative revisions in earnings forecasts and equity price declines.
candidate_trigger: 3-month moving average of INDPRO is below its 12-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:Recent industrial production data has been flat to slightly negative.
failure_condition: The signal's information coefficient (IC) for predicting negative 60-day returns remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong, persistent growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates a healthy and expanding economy, which boosts investor confidence and expectations for future corporate earnings growth, driving positive short-term equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:The most recent PAYEMS report showed a strong beat vs expectations.
failure_condition: The average 20-day forward return following a top-quartile monthly PAYEMS change is not statistically different from the average return following a bottom-quartile change.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts, a key interest-rate sensitive sector, signals that restrictive monetary policy is effectively curbing economic activity. This serves as a leading indicator for a broader slowdown, negatively impacting equities.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month average is down >10% YoY AND FEDFUNDS > 4.0%
failure_condition: During historical tightening cycles, periods meeting the trigger condition are not followed by a negative median 60-day S&P 500 return.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds rate is held at a high and stable level, the restrictive policy stance continues to weigh on economic growth and corporate profitability over time, creating a headwind for equity returns over a multi-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS has been unchanged for at least 3 consecutive months and is above 4.5%
evidence_refs: fact:The Fed has held rates steady at 5.33% for several months.
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward return during periods that meet the trigger condition is positive over the last three FOMC tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: An extremely low unemployment rate, while seemingly positive, indicates a tight labor market that can lead to wage-push inflation. This forces the central bank to maintain a hawkish stance, which is a negative for equities over the very short term as rate cut expectations are diminished.
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return is positive during historical periods when unemployment is below the 3.7% threshold.
